fields.description:
# op-cert: AWS IaC + deploy of the trusted gate service (zero fixed-cost: existing VM + API Gateway SigV4) + live authenticated integrity test

## Why
The trusted gate service (the sibling service story) is only a trust anchor once it runs in the
controlled environment with SigV4-authenticated access and a key nobody else can read. This
story declares that as code, **actually deploys it** (an authenticated AWS CLI is provided — the
deploy is in scope), and proves the deployed endpoint end-to-end. Deployment decisions are
settled in the epic's "Producer-signing & compute-runtime design resolution", **cost-verified
against the live account**: rebar's entire AWS footprint is the one t4g.large `rebar-gerrit`
box (+ Route 53 + free SSM parameters), so the binding constraint is **zero increase in fixed
monthly cost** — the service rides the existing box (the compose slot freed by the autolander's
retirement, ADR 0047) behind an API Gateway HTTP API (pay-per-request, no fixed fee), with the
key in free SSM Parameter Store. A dedicated Fargate/NLB/Secrets-Manager/KMS-CMK stack was
rejected (~$50+/mo new spend for isolation ADR 0046 L4 does not require).

**Hard dependency (not yet on `main`):** the sibling service story provides
`src/rebar/opcert_service/` and `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` — neither exists on `main`
yet; this story starts only after that story lands (enforced by the `depends_on` link).

## What
- **Terraform — extend the existing `infra/terraform/`** (the module that already declares the
  Gerrit EC2, its IAM instance profile, and the data volume):
  - an **API Gateway HTTP API** (v2) whose routes set `authorization_type = "AWS_IAM"` (SigV4
    — a ROUTE attribute; HTTP API v2 has NO separate `aws_apigatewayv2_authorizer` resource for
    IAM), over TLS. The integration URI is **`https://` to the box's HOST-nginx TLS origin**
    (nginx + certbot run as HOST services on the box — `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` header —
    with TLS on 443 at the Elastic-IP-bound hostname; NOT `http://…:80`, which the existing
    config 301-redirects and would break the integration) for the `/opcert/` routes, injecting a shared origin-guard header. Caller restriction is
    identity-policy-based (an HTTP API has no resource policy): `AWS_IAM` auth requires SigV4,
    and the IaC CREATES the admin role it restricts to — `aws_iam_role` **`rebar-opcert-admin`**
    (no such role pre-exists in `infra/terraform/`), trust policy limited to the principal ARNs
    supplied via a new variable `opcert_admin_principal_arns` (set at deploy to the operator's
    IAM principals), with an inline policy granting `execute-api:Invoke` scoped to exactly this
    API's execution ARN — and no other principal/policy in the IaC grants Invoke;
  - an **SSM Parameter Store SecureString** `/rebar/prod/opcert-ed25519-key` (declared with a
    placeholder `value` + `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }`, so the out-of-band-seeded
    key is never reverted or clobbered by a later `terraform apply`) holding the
    **passphrase-free Ed25519 private key**. NO new IAM
    grant: the parameter lives under `/rebar/prod/`, which the instance profile's existing
    `rebar-gerrit-ssm-params-read` policy (`infra/terraform/iam.tf`) already covers — and
    nothing broader is added;
  - the **origin-guard header provisioning path**: Terraform generates the guard value with
    `random_password` — the `hashicorp/random` provider is ADDED to
    `infra/terraform/versions.tf` `required_providers` (today it pins only `hashicorp/aws
    ~> 5.0`; `terraform init` refreshes `.terraform.lock.hcl`) — stores it as SSM SecureString
    `/rebar/prod/opcert-origin-guard` (also under the existing grant), and maps it as a static
    request-header parameter (`X-Opcert-Guard`) on the API Gateway integration. Unlike the KEY
    parameter, the guard parameter is fully Terraform-managed and carries NO
    `lifecycle ignore_changes` (its value IS `random_password.result`; rotation =
    `terraform apply -replace=` on the random resource, which updates the SSM value and the
    integration header together) — the `ignore_changes = [value]` guard applies ONLY to the
    operator-seeded Ed25519 key parameter;
  - NO new fixed-monthly-cost resources: no Fargate/ECS service, no load balancer/VPC link, no
    Secrets Manager secret, no customer KMS CMK, and no `kms:Sign` anywhere (SSHSIG signing is
    performed by the service itself; AWS KMS has no Ed25519 signing).
- **Compose — extend `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`**: an `opcert` service on
  `infra/compose/Dockerfile.opcert` (sibling story) with env `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` and the SSM
  parameter names; an nginx route `/opcert/` → the service that rejects requests whose
  `X-Opcert-Guard` header does not match. On the box the guard value is materialized from its
  SSM parameter by `infra/scripts/materialize-opcert-guard.sh` into a HOST-nginx include
  (nginx runs as a host service, not a container) with NAMED files: the script writes
  `/etc/nginx/opcert-guard.conf` (`map $http_x_opcert_guard $opcert_guard_ok { default 0;
  "<value>" 1; }`), and `infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template` — which today has NO `/opcert/`
  location or include — gains both the `include /etc/nginx/opcert-guard.conf;` directive and a
  `location /opcert/ { if ($opcert_guard_ok = 0) { return 403; } proxy_pass … }` block routing
  to the compose service; the guard value also lands in the service env. Two ordering hazards are closed
  explicitly: (a) BOOT — host nginx (systemd) loads `rebar.conf` (and its include) BEFORE
  `compose-up.sh` ever runs, so the nginx-config install step that ships `rebar.conf` from the
  template ALSO installs a DENY-ALL default `/etc/nginx/opcert-guard.conf`
  (`map $http_x_opcert_guard $opcert_guard_ok { default 0; }`) — a fresh boot is fail-closed
  (403 on /opcert/) rather than nginx failing to start on a missing include; the materialize
  script then overwrites it with the real map and reloads; (b) ROTATION — the documented
  runbook is two steps in order: `terraform apply -replace=random_password.opcert_guard`
  (updates the SSM value + the API Gateway static header), then re-run
  `materialize-opcert-guard.sh` (rewrites the nginx map + `nginx -s reload`); between the two
  steps /opcert/ serves 403 — fail-closed, a brief and acceptable rotation window following the existing
  precedent (`infra/gerrit/materialize-deploy-key.sh` / `materialize-g2p-config.sh` — SSM →
  file). It is WIRED INTO the boot orchestrator `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` alongside the
  existing materialize/.env-regeneration calls, running BEFORE `docker compose up` (the
  precedent ordering), and it finishes with `nginx -s reload` so a guard rotation re-run takes
  effect on the host nginx (at first boot the reload is a no-op-safe refresh) — the same value Terraform maps onto the API Gateway integration, closing
  the loop.
- **Deploy + prove** (authenticated AWS CLI): `terraform apply`; generate the key locally
  (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -N ""`) and seed the SecureString (the key exists nowhere else);
  deploy the compose service on the box; pin the environment's public key + `env_id` into
  `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml` per the epic's "Authoritative era anchor" resolution
  (`added_at_log_position`, operator-stamped at the current tickets tip); then the live checks — a
  SigV4-signed job round-trip (submit → poll → terminal status), an unsigned request rejected
  (401/403 at API Gateway), and a direct-to-origin request without the guard header rejected.
## Scope
- In: the Terraform extension, compose/nginx wiring, key generation + SSM seeding procedure,
  the real deploy, the live round-trip evidence, the trusted-env pinning.
- Out: the service code and Dockerfile (sibling story); any change to rebar library code; the
  merge-gate consumer (shipped, 4214); resizing or re-homing the existing box.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/terraform/` declares: the API Gateway HTTP API (v2) with
      `authorization_type = "AWS_IAM"` on its routes over TLS,
      `execute-api:Invoke` granted only to the IaC-created `rebar-opcert-admin` role (trust policy
      from `opcert_admin_principal_arns`; the assertion method is STRUCTURED and SELF-FAILING —
      the literal command the test runs exits non-zero on any violation:
      `terraform plan -out=tf.plan && terraform show -json tf.plan | jq -e
      '[.planned_values.root_module.resources[] | select(.type == "aws_iam_role_policy" or
      .type == "aws_iam_policy") | {label: .name, s: (.values.policy | fromjson | .Statement[])}
      | select([.s.Action] | flatten | any(. == "execute-api:Invoke"))] | length == 1 and
      .[0].label == "opcert_admin_invoke"'` — where `.name` in `terraform show -json` is the
      Terraform RESOURCE LABEL, so the invoke policy is declared with the pinned label
      `aws_iam_role_policy.opcert_admin_invoke` on the `rebar-opcert-admin` role; exactly one
      statement grants Invoke and it is that resource, else the command exits non-zero), the `hashicorp/random` provider in `required_providers`,
      the box-origin integration with the static `X-Opcert-Guard` request-header mapping; and
      the two SecureString parameters (the key parameter guarded by
      `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }`)
      (`/rebar/prod/opcert-ed25519-key`, `/rebar/prod/opcert-origin-guard`) under the existing
      `rebar-gerrit-ssm-params-read` path grant with NO new/broader IAM grant. A test asserts
      these declarations exist.
- [ ] `terraform fmt -check` and `terraform validate` pass (asserted by a test or CI step);
      `terraform plan` against the provided account succeeds.
- [ ] `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` + nginx config gain the `opcert` service and the
      guarded `/opcert/` route; a test asserts the service entry, the env wiring
      (`REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID`, parameter names), that the nginx route requires the
      `X-Opcert-Guard` header, and that the materialize script (per the `infra/gerrit/materialize-*.sh`
      precedent) renders the guard value from its SSM parameter — tested hermetically with a
      stub `aws` executable prepended to `PATH` that returns a fixture value (no live SSM or
      account access in unit tests). The test also asserts `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` calls
      the materialize script before `docker compose up`, that the script ends with
      `nginx -s reload` (host nginx), and that the Terraform integration URI uses the
      `https://` scheme.
- [ ] The deployed environment's public key and `env_id` are pinned in
      `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml` (with `added_at_log_position` per the epic's SUPERSEDING "Authoritative era anchor" resolution — the tickets-branch log-position era fields, not the superseded `added_at_commit` main-SHA form); a
      test asserts the entry parses and the `env_id` matches the compose service's
      `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The service is deployed from `infra/` via the authenticated AWS CLI
      and the live checks pass: a SigV4-signed job round-trip succeeds; an unsigned request is
      rejected at API Gateway; a direct-to-origin request without the guard header is rejected —
      evidence: a comment recording the `terraform apply` outputs (API endpoint id/URL) and the
      captured round-trip + both rejections.
